operationalSituations.description: Normal vehicle operation — all zone controllers reachable
Partial network fault — one or more zone controllers unreachable
Safety-critical manoeuvre — emergency braking or collision avoidance active
HPC software fault — runaway process, crash, or OOM condition
Elevated network latency — congestion, EMI, or hardware degradation
Adversarial access — attacker present on the zone Ethernet bus

hazards.description: Loss of command delivery to safety-critical zone (e.g. braking actuator)
Spurious command sent to wrong zone controller
Zone controller watchdog not kicked, leading to unintended reset
Replay of stale commands from a previous drive cycle
Zone controller falsely reported as alive when unresponsive
Priority inversion — low-priority burst of commands starves a safety-critical CmdWatchdog
Rate limiter blocks watchdog kick during high command burst, causing spurious zone reset
Unauthorized command injection via unsecured UDP transport on the zone Ethernet bus
Zone controller not properly woken from sleep, causing loss of actuation during safety manoeuvre
Fault injection state persists across vehicle power cycles, masking real faults in production

safetyGoals.description: Commands to safety-critical zones shall be delivered within the watchdog period or a fault shall be signalled.
Commands shall only be processed by the zone they are addressed to; misrouted commands shall be rejected.
The watchdog kick command (CmdWatchdog) shall always be deliverable at the configured priority.
Replayed or duplicated commands from prior sessions shall be detected and rejected.
A zone controller that stops publishing Status shall be detected as dead within the configured deadline.
Priority::Critical commands shall never be delayed by Priority::Normal or Priority::High commands queued earlier.
Transport authentication (mTLS or equivalent) shall be enforced on all external zone controller connections.
A zone controller shall only be declared operational after a successful Wake command response.
Fault injection rules shall not persist beyond the lifetime of the injecting process.
Zone controller health state transitions shall be deterministically derivable from observable send() outcomes alone.
